data.body:
PASS-2 independent verification (findings 12–13), re-grounded against the live ticket graph (this epic 4fcd; children 22bf, 5a6c; sibling-bug a36b) and attest.py.

[12] CLAIM: child a36b expresses an "ANY new commit (even unrelated) invalidates" REQUIREMENT that the plan contradicts by narrowing to material-only drift. VERDICT: NOT ENTAILED — citation inverted. a36b ("worm-folly-barge") is a BUG ticket whose title/description name the any-commit-invalidates behavior as a DEFECT TO FIX (over-invalidation friction), NOT a requirement to preserve: description "ANY subsequent commit — even one in a completely unrelated file — invalidates a freshly-earned, still-correct attestation"; Expected "The claim succeeds: the attestation is still valid because none of the code ticketA's plan was grounded against changed"; AC1 "Claim freshness is scoped to the code the review was grounded against (resolved edit-set / cited files) rather than the whole HEAD sha, so an unrelated commit does NOT stale the attestation." That is the SAME material-only scoping the plan adopts — the plan AGREES with a36b. The "ANY new commit invalidates" behavior survives only as the empty-set CONSERVATIVE FALLBACK in sibling story 22bf (unscopable tickets), not a broad dropped requirement. The finding reads the child's intent backwards. Duplicates prior PASS-2 finding [10] already on this ticket.

[13] CLAIM: child 5a6c's narrowed delta-re-review decision is silently dropped (plan "only says drift prompts re-review"). VERDICT: NOT ENTAILED. 5a6c ("grace-thud-feint") is a LIVE OPEN STORY under THIS epic (parent_id=4fcd-449a-d1b3-4a23; depends_on 22bf), titled "Narrowed delta-re-review on code drift: re-run only the enforced code-grounded criteria with a chained attestation," carrying the full decision (re-run only {P2,P3} ∪ applicable CODEBASE_GROUNDED, reuse cached findings, chained attestation, fail-safe to full re-review on version skew). The decomposition comment states "Story 2 grace-thud-feint (5a6c, depends_on S1) = narrowed delta-re-review." A refinement decomposed into a dependency-sequenced child story is NOT silently dropped; the epic's 3 ACs are intentionally the minimal success contract. Duplicates prior PASS-2 finding [11].

ROOT ERROR (both): treating a child ticket's title/existence as an externally-expressed requirement the plan contradicts/omits, when the children ARE the plan's own decomposition resolving these very points (22bf+5a6c implement; a36b is the sibling bug whose fix the plan adopts). cited_reference_accurate: [12]=no (a36b says the inverse of the claim); [13]=na (5a6c correctly named but the dropped-inference is wrong).
